title: The Adventures of Toto – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Why did Grandfather buy Toto from the tonga-driver?
questions.answer: Grandfather bought Toto because the monkey looked out of place tied to a feeding trough and he wanted to add him to his private zoo.

questions.question: How is Toto described as a pretty monkey?
questions.answer: Toto had bright mischievous eyes, pearly white teeth, and a long tail that worked like a third hand.

questions.question: Why was Toto kept secretly in the house?
questions.answer: Toto was kept secretly because Grandmother disliked Grandfather bringing new animals home.

questions.question: What happened when Toto was kept in the closet?
questions.answer: Toto pulled out the peg, tore the wallpaper, and shredded the narrator’s blazer.

questions.question: Why was Toto shifted to the servants’ quarters?
questions.answer: He was shifted to keep him with the other pets in a cage.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather take Toto to Saharanpur?
questions.answer: Grandfather went to Saharanpur to collect his pension and took Toto along.

questions.question: How did Toto behave inside the canvas bag?
questions.answer: He kept jumping and rolling inside the bag, attracting people’s attention.

questions.question: Why did the ticket collector charge fare for Toto?
questions.answer: The ticket collector classified Toto as a dog and demanded a fare.

questions.question: How did Grandfather try to prove his point to the ticket collector?
questions.answer: He showed his pet tortoise and asked if it should also be charged.

questions.question: Where was Toto finally kept after Grandmother accepted him?
questions.answer: Toto was kept in the stable with the family donkey Nana.

questions.question: How did Toto trouble Nana?
questions.answer: He bit Nana’s ears with his sharp teeth.

questions.question: How did Toto learn to bathe like a human?
questions.answer: He imitated the narrator by watching him bathe.

questions.question: What happened when Toto felt hurt during bath time?
questions.answer: He stopped bathing if someone laughed at him.

questions.question: How did Toto almost boil himself alive?
questions.answer: He got into a kettle of warm water placed on the fire for tea.

questions.question: Why did Toto keep hopping in the kettle?
questions.answer: Because the water kept getting hotter.

questions.question: What did Toto do at the dining table during lunch?
questions.answer: He began stuffing himself with rice from the dish of pullao.

questions.question: How did Toto react when Grandmother screamed?
questions.answer: He threw a plate at her.

questions.question: Where did Toto run with the dish of pullao?
questions.answer: He ran to the branches of the jackfruit tree.

questions.question: What did Toto do after finishing the rice?
questions.answer: He threw the dish down from the tree.

questions.question: Why did Toto throw the dish down?
questions.answer: He did it to spite Grandmother.

questions.question: Why was Toto not suitable as a pet?
questions.answer: Because he caused constant destruction and trouble.

questions.question: What kind of damage did Toto cause frequently?
questions.answer: He destroyed dishes, clothes, curtains, and wallpaper.

questions.question: Why could the family not keep Toto for long?
questions.answer: They could not afford the damage he caused.

questions.question: What does Toto’s behaviour reveal about monkeys?
questions.answer: Monkeys are curious, intelligent, and mischievous animals.

questions.question: What type of story is 'The Adventures of Toto'?
questions.answer: It is a humorous story about a mischievous pet monkey.
